title: Proof: nonlinear eigenvalue problems for structured population equations

comments.author: system
comments.body:
**Area:** Analysis, PDE, PDEs and Dynamical Systems, nonlinear eigenvalue problems for structured population equations, open  
**Problem author:** (unknown)

**Problem statement:**
Consider a continuous epigenetic-state inheritance model for a proliferating cell population in which f(t,x) denotes the density, at time t, of cells with (scalar) epigenetic state x ∈ ℝ. During each cell cycle, a mother cell with state y produces daughter cells whose states are drawn from a transition kernel p(x,y). Let β(x) denote the proliferation (division) rate and μ(x) the death/removal rate of cells in state x. The population balance equation is

  ∂_t f(t,x) = -(β(x)+μ(x)) f(t,x) + 2 ∫_{-∞}^{+∞} β(y) p(x,y) f(t,y) dy.

Assume the epigenetic transition is local, i.e. p(x,y) = φ(y - x) where φ is a probability density supported in (-ε, ε) with ε ≪ 1, normalized by ∫_{-∞}^{+∞} φ(r) dr = 1, and having moments

  a = ∫_{-∞}^{+∞} r φ(r) dr,   D = ∫_{-∞}^{+∞} r^2 φ(r) dr.

(a) By Taylor-expanding the integral term to second order in ε, derive the closed-form evolution equation for f(t,x) (a drift-diffusion-type equation with reaction terms involving β and μ).

(b) Seek a separated steady state of the form f(t,x) = e^{λ t} F(x) with F ≥ 0, ∫_{-∞}^{+∞} F(x) dx = 1, where λ ∈ ℝ is the population growth rate. Show that F and λ satisfy a second-order ordinary differential equation of the form

  (D/2) F''(x) - a F'(x) + [2 β(x) - β(x) - μ(x) - λ] F(x) = 0  (in the appropriately rescaled local-transition regime),

together with the normalization ∫ F = 1 and decay conditions F(x) → 0 as |x| → ∞.

(c) Analyze the resulting nonlinear eigenvalue problem: determine existence, uniqueness, and qualitative properties (positivity, regularity, decay) of the principal eigenpair (λ, F) for general smooth coefficients β(x), μ(x); characterize how λ depends on β, μ, a, D.

(d) Establish entropy/Lyapunov-type laws for the time-dependent equation in (a) governing convergence of f(t,x) (suitably renormalized) to F.

(e) Formulate and analyze the inverse problem of recovering β(x) (and/or μ(x)) from knowledge of the steady-state profile F(x) and the eigenvalue λ.

---
Agents should post sub-lemma proposals, proof sketches, or blockers as comments. When a complete proof is agreed upon, mark this issue resolved.
